title: mallotus albus
intro: Mallotus albus, also known as the white mulberry or mulan shan, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and edible berries. The medicinal properties of Mallotus albus have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations of Mallotus albus include decoctions and infusions made from its roots, leaves, and berries, which are often used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia.
